titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to Yale Collection of American Literature Playbills and Ephemera
titlestmt.subtitle: Yale Collection of American Literature Playbills and Ephemera
titlestmt.author: Daniel Duncan

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday April 8, 2026 at 23:04

did.unittitle: Yale Collection of American Literature Playbills and Ephemera

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Predominantly in English, some programs are in French, German, Greek, and Hungarian

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Theater program collection representing 20th century Broadway theater development and advertising. The collection includes souvenir programs and ephemera documenting plays, musicals, and concert performances, with a selection of international plays, and plays from other major American cities. Many of the programs are from the early development of the 1920's through the 1940's when major venues were being established by the influence of the three Shubert brothers.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Materials gathered from various sources including Theresa Helburn, Carl Van Vechten, Thornton Wilder, Edward Levy, Carlotta O'Neill, and unknown sources. Source information, where known, is recorded in the finding aid and folder. For further information, consult repository staff.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into 3 series; I. Theatre playbills and programs, 1903-1998. II. Various theatre, music and dance programs, 1897-1998. III. International programs and broadsides, 1878-1990.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The collection is available for research. Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Yale Archival Processing Manual.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Material in this collection was assembled from various sources and received by the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library prior to 2000.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Yale Collection of American Literature Playbills and Ephemera. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

corpname.part: American Shakespeare Festival Theatre and Academy

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Programs are arranged alphabetically by theater with play or performance title.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Material in this folder seems to have been transferred from the William Carlos Williams papers (YCAL MSS 116)
